I'd rather them have used two or three more episodes to tell a complete story rather than setting up a season of knight-in-shining-plane off to rescue damsel-who's-not-in-distress.
label: critique

text: Episode 7 is genuinely one of the best episodes of television that I've ever seen. That being said, I was absolutely gobsmacked at how stupid the season finale was. The prior episodes established the bunker was the biggest secret in the world, yet the government uses regular construction crews? A recycling facility was the best cover they could come up with? The presidential assassination conspiracy being undone by an allergy to tree nuts is horrifically lazy writing.
label: critique

text: A little bit. I expected a little more twist and turn, and a little more suspense. Some of the story was a little sloppier than past episodes. But it was decent. I don't really buy the girl assassin being as hard core and psycho as they are presenting her.

text: Tried too hard for a plot twist by being a character that had nothing to do with the previous 7 episodes. Makes no sense to the build up created and he doesn't even show him outside.

text: Disappointed? I'm sorry but I thought it was brilliant. text: honestly a terrible last episode. A random builder hurt because his colleagues were left behind decided to kill the president one day and did it with no one suspecting it. text: Yeah, the first 70% of the season was really intriguing. The ending feels like a subversion of expectations for the sake of subversion of expectations. text: Halfway through episode three and needed to come to Reddit for validation that I'm not just a snob, this show actually does have pretty bad writing.
